Snowflake, Palantir Form Partnership for Enterprise AI
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller


Snowflake and Palantir formed a new partnership focused on enabling enterprises to build artificial intelligence applications, faster data analytics and more efficient and trusted data pipelines.

Snowflake shares rose 6.7% in premarket trading while Palantir ticked up 1.2%.

As part of the partnership, Snowflake is integrating its AI Data Cloud platform with Palantir's Foundry operating system and its Artificial Intelligence Platform, which connects AI with enterprises' data and operations.

Joint customers of the two companies will be able to have two-way interoperability between the companies' platforms.

"Bringing the power of the Palantir and Snowflake platforms together is a natural fit, aimed at reducing friction for customers to more easily deploy intelligent apps and accelerate time to value," Snowflake Chief Revenue Officer Mike Gannon.


Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-16-25 0756ET

Zee Entertainment Enterprises Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Second Quarter and Six Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Zee Entertainment Enterprises Limited reported earnings results for the second quarter and six months ended September 30, 2025. For the second quarter, the company reported sales was INR 1,399 million compared to INR 1,291 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 19,956 million compared to INR 20,344 million a year ago. Net income was INR 765 million compared to INR 2,094 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 0.79 compared to INR 2.18 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 0.79 compared to INR 2.18 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was INR 0.79 compared to INR 2.18 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was INR 0.79 compared to INR 2.18 a year ago.
For the six months, sales was INR 2,245 million compared to INR 3,611 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 38,454 million compared to INR 41,839 million a year ago. Net income was INR 2,202 million compared to INR 3,275 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 2.29 compared to INR 3.49 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 2.29 compared to INR 3.49 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was INR 2.29 compared to INR 3.41 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was INR 2.29 compared to INR 3.41 a year ago.

data.title: Infosys Limited Declares Interim Dividend, Payable on November 7, 2025
data.description: Infosys Limited announced that at its meeting held on October 16, 2025, shareholders declared an interim dividend of INR 23/- per equity share, fixed October 27, 2025 as a record date and November 7, 2025 as a payout date.

India's Cyient posts 40% profit drop in seasonally challenging second-quarter
data.description:
(Reuters) -India' Cyient on Thursday reported a nearly 40% fall in profit, as a higher wage bill in a seasonally tough quarter weighed on the engineering research and development firm.

Consolidated net profit came in at 1.28 billion rupees ($14 million), down from 1.79 billion rupees last year. 

Revenue remained largely flat, down 1% to 18.31 billion rupees, from 18.49 billion rupees a year ago. 

Cyient had changed the way it reports its segment-wise revenue from the last quarter onwards, after it carved out its semiconductor business into a separate subsidiary unit.

KEY CONTEXT

While U.S. policy shifts - including President Donald Trump's proposed H-1B visa changes - have rattled India's IT services sector, Cyient has stayed relatively insulated, thanks to its limited overseas workforce and focus on project delivery from India. 

The company, which counts Honeywell and Bharat Electronics among its clients, had deployed just six workers on H-1B visas in FY25, and does not anticipate material impact on its financials for FY26, the company said in September.

Still, supply chain bottlenecks have weighed on its engineering R&D operations, particularly in its transport, mobility, and semiconductor businesses.

President and chief financial officer Prabhakar Atla highlighted headwinds from wage hikes as well as ongoing macroeconomic uncertainties, as some reasons for the company's performance this quarter.

Peer Tata Elxsi reported a drop in profit for the fourth straight quarter last week, as its key transportation business declined.

data.title: UK's Reeves says she would like a bigger fiscal buffer but that means trade-offs
data.description:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) -British finance minister Rachel Reeves said on Thursday she would like to have a bigger fiscal buffer to cope with volatile global markets, but that creating one in her budget next month would involve trade-offs on tax and spending.

"Would I like more headroom? Of course I would. But that does come with trade-offs because more headroom requires more tax revenue or less spending on public services like the NHS, so you've got to get the balance right there," Reeves told reporters.

On Wednesday she said she was looking at tax and spending measures for her next budget on November 26, having raised taxes by 40 billion pounds in her first fiscal plan last year.

(Reporting by David Milliken in WashingtonWriting by William James and editing by William Schomberg in London)
